rulings.Case Title: ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL VS. NEWREZ, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 7, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT NEWREZ LLC, F/K/A NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is moot, as the demurrer is sustained in its entirety. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL VS. NEWREZ, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 7, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT NEWREZ LLC, F/K/A NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. The Court had previously sustained the demurrer to the First Amendment Complaint as to the causes of action subject to this demurrer with instructions to allege facts regarding materiality of the violations. Plaintiff failed to do so. The addition of the allegations that "[s]pecifically, Defendant's violations resulted in Plaintiff's application never being considered and/or in being denied when Plaintiff was eligible for a modification" did not cure the defect. This is a conclusory statement, not a factual allegation. Therefore, Plaintiff shall have a 10-day leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of each cause of action including facts in support of materiality. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL, LP ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 7, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO ASSESSOR-RECORDER, SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 5th, 7th and 10th causes of action. Non-opposition filed. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action. San Francisco Business and Tax Regulations Code 1114(b). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action for Plaintiff to allege a claim per Todd Shipyards Corp. v. City of Los Angeles (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 222, 225-227 and Ball v. Cty. of Los Angeles (1978) 82 Cal.App.3d 312, 315, if Plaintiff can allege facts giving rise to a claim for interest on a refund. If Plaintiff is seeking a refund on the interest paid, such claims are subsumed in the second cause of action, which is not subject to this demurrer. Demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. Whether the City's tax penalties are excessive and violate constitutional rights is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. The Court notes however, that none of the authorities relied upon by the Plaintiff in opposition support Plaintiff legal position that the penalty is excessive. Demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The Court declines Plaintiff's invitation to disregard CIM Urban REIT 211 Main St. v. City & Cnty. of San Francisco (2022) 75 Cal.App.5th 939. Demurrer to the eighth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting Plaintiff's assertion that the proceeds of the transfer tax are earmarked/dedicated/required to be used for a specific project. Demurrer to the Ninth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the position of the Government Claim putting the City on notice of the violation of the Dormant Commerce Clause and any taxes paid in other jurisdictions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
